**HARGROVE SECURITIES LLC** 405 Park Avenue South, 22nd Floor New York, New York 10016

November 8, 2024

CONFIDENTIAL

Board of Directors Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc. 4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800 Austin, Texas 78759

Attention: Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy, Chairman & Chief Executive Officer

cc: David Nishimura, Chief Financial Officer Samantha Reeves, General Counsel & Secretary

Re: Engagement Letter --- Initial Public Offering of Common Stock

Dear Members of the Board of Directors:

**[1. Introduction and Engagement]{.underline}**

Hargrove Securities LLC (\"Hargrove\" or the \"Lead Book-Runner\") is pleased to set forth the terms upon which Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the \"Company\"), has engaged Hargrove to act as lead book-running manager in connection with a proposed initial public offering (the \"Offering\") of shares of the Company\'s common stock, par value \$0.001 per share (the \"Common Stock\"), on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the proposed ticker symbol \"MPLS.\"

Hargrove will serve as lead book-running manager for the Offering. Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc. (\"Bellweather\"), a Massachusetts corporation with offices at 150 Federal Street, Suite 3100, Boston, MA 02110, will serve as co-managing underwriter. Hargrove and Bellweather are referred to herein collectively as the \"Underwriters.\" The Offering will be conducted pursuant to a registration statement on Form S-1 (the \"Registration Statement\") to be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the \"SEC\").

The Company\'s outside counsel is Stonebridge & Calloway LLP, 600 Congress Avenue, Suite 2400, Austin, TX 78701 (lead partner: Jonathan Calloway) (\"Company Counsel\"). The Underwriters\' counsel will be Ashford & Pine LLP, 1231 Avenue of the Americas, 34th Floor, New York, NY 10020 (lead partner: Victoria Ashford) (\"Underwriters\' Counsel\"). The Company\'s independent registered public accounting firm is Whitman Reese & Co., 200 S. Wacker Drive, Suite 1500, Chicago, IL 60606 (engagement partner: Gregory Whitman, CPA).

Cameron Whitfield, Managing Director, Equity Capital Markets, will oversee the transaction for Hargrove. Lisa Tanaka, Vice President, Equity Capital Markets, will serve as the day-to-day contact for the engagement at Hargrove. Patrick Donnelly, Managing Director at Bellweather, will be the co-manager contact.

This engagement letter (this \"Letter\") supersedes all prior discussions, correspondence, and agreements between the parties regarding the subject matter hereof and is subject in all respects to the negotiation, execution, and delivery of a definitive underwriting agreement (the \"Underwriting Agreement\") among the Company, the Selling Stockholders (as defined below), and the Underwriters.

**[2. Offering Structure]{.underline}**

The proposed structure of the Offering is as follows:

**Type.** The Offering will be a firm commitment underwriting for the initial public offering of shares of Common Stock.

**Company Shares (Primary).** The Company will offer and sell 8,000,000 newly issued shares of Common Stock (the \"Primary Shares\").

**Selling Stockholder Shares (Secondary).** The following stockholders of the Company (the \"Selling Stockholders\") will offer and sell shares of Common Stock in the Offering as follows:

> \(a\) Cascade Kestridge Ventures, LP, a Delaware limited partnership: 2,000,000 shares;
>
> \(b\) Northlight Growth Partners Fund II, LP, a Delaware limited partnership: 1,500,000 shares; and
>
> \(c\) Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy: up to 750,000 shares.

The shares offered by the Selling Stockholders are referred to as the \"Secondary Shares.\" The Secondary Shares are expected to total 4,000,000 shares for purposes of baseline economic calculations set forth in Section 3 of this Letter, recognizing that the Secondary Shares may total up to 4,250,000 shares given the \"up to\" nature of Dr. Krishnamurthy\'s participation.

**Total Firm Shares.** The total number of firm shares to be offered in the Offering (the \"Firm Shares\") shall be 12,000,000 shares, consisting of 8,000,000 Primary Shares and 4,000,000 Secondary Shares (at the baseline assumption).

**Over-Allotment Option (Greenshoe).** The Company will grant to the Underwriters an option (the \"Over-Allotment Option\"), exercisable within 30 calendar days of the Closing Date (as defined below), to purchase up to 1,800,000 additional shares of Common Stock (equal to 15% of the Firm Shares) at the public offering price less the underwriting discount. The over-allotment option shares shall be sourced exclusively from newly issued Company shares.

**Maximum Total Shares.** Including the full exercise of the Over-Allotment Option, the maximum total number of shares offered in the Offering shall be 13,800,000 shares.

**Expected Price Range.** The expected price range for the Offering is \$22.00 to \$26.00 per share (the \"Price Range\"). For purposes of this Letter, the assumed initial public offering price is \$24.00 per share (the midpoint of the Price Range) (the \"Assumed IPO Price\").

**Listing.** Application will be made to list the Common Stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the proposed ticker symbol \"MPLS.\"

**Registration.** The Registration Statement will be filed on Form S-1 with the SEC under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the \"Securities Act\"). An SEC File Number will be assigned upon filing.

**[3. Pricing and Economics]{.underline}**

**3.1 Public Offering Price and Underwriting Discount**

The public offering price per share (the \"Public Offering Price\") will be determined by agreement between the Company and the Lead Book-Runner based on prevailing market conditions, investor demand, and other relevant factors at the time of pricing and is expected to be within the Price Range of \$22.00 to \$26.00 per share.

The underwriting discount will be 6.0% of the Public Offering Price per share (the \"Underwriting Discount\"). At the Assumed IPO Price of \$24.00 per share, the Underwriting Discount equals \$1.44 per share. The net price to the Company per Primary Share and the net price to each Selling Stockholder per Secondary Share shall be \$22.56 per share (i.e., \$24.00 minus \$1.44).

**3.2 Gross and Net Proceeds Estimates**

The following estimates are based on the Assumed IPO Price of \$24.00 per share and a baseline assumption of 12,000,000 Firm Shares. All figures are estimates and are subject to adjustment at the time of pricing.

**Gross Proceeds.** Gross proceeds from the Firm Shares: 12,000,000 × \$24.00 = \$288,000,000. Gross proceeds if the Over-Allotment Option is fully exercised: 13,800,000 × \$24.00 = \$331,200,000.

**Total Underwriting Discount.** Total Underwriting Discount on the Firm Shares: 12,000,000 × \$1.44 = \$17,280,000. Total Underwriting Discount if the Over-Allotment Option is fully exercised: 13,800,000 × \$1.44 = \$19,872,000.

**Net Proceeds to the Company.** Net proceeds to the Company from Primary Shares: 8,000,000 × \$22.56 = \$180,480,000. Net proceeds to the Company if the Over-Allotment Option is fully exercised (all greenshoe shares being newly issued Company shares): 9,800,000 × \$22.56 = \$221,088,000.

**Net Proceeds to Selling Stockholders.** Net proceeds to the Selling Stockholders (in total): 4,000,000 × \$22.56 = \$90,240,000, allocated as follows:

> • Cascade Kestridge Ventures, LP: 2,000,000 × \$22.56 = \$45,120,000
>
> • Northlight Growth Partners Fund II, LP: 1,500,000 × \$22.56 = \$33,840,000
>
> • Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy: 500,000 × \$22.56 = \$11,280,000

All figures set forth in this Section 3.2 are estimates based on the Assumed IPO Price and the baseline share assumptions described in Section 2 and are subject to adjustment at the time of pricing.

**3.3 Syndicate Allocation**

The allocation of the Firm Shares and Over-Allotment Option shares among the Underwriters shall be as follows:

> • Hargrove Securities LLC: 70% of Firm Shares (8,400,000 shares) and 70% of Over-Allotment Option shares (1,260,000 shares).
>
> • Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc.: 30% of Firm Shares (3,600,000 shares) and 30% of Over-Allotment Option shares (540,000 shares).

The Over-Allotment Option shares shall be allocated among the Underwriters on the same pro rata basis as the Firm Shares.

**[4. Anticipated Timeline and Key Milestones]{.underline}**

The following is an indicative timeline for the Offering. All dates are targets and are subject to change based on SEC review, market conditions, and other factors.

> • **S-1 Filing with SEC:** Q1 2025 (target: January 2025). The Registration Statement will be filed under an SEC File Number to be assigned.
>
> • **SEC Review Period and Amendments:** Approximately 6--10 weeks following initial filing for SEC staff comment letters and responsive amendments.
>
> • **Target Effectiveness of Registration Statement:** March 2025.
>
> • **Road Show:** Approximately 7--10 business days, to commence immediately following effectiveness of the Registration Statement (or declaration of effectiveness by the SEC).
>
> • **Pricing Date:** Target March 2025 (the \"Pricing Date\").
>
> • **Closing Date:** T+3 business days after the Pricing Date (the \"Closing Date\"), consistent with IPO settlement conventions.
>
> • **Over-Allotment Option Exercise Period:** 30 calendar days following the Closing Date.

The Company\'s independent registered public accounting firm, Whitman Reese & Co., will deliver a comfort letter addressed to the Underwriters prior to the Pricing Date and a bring-down comfort letter at the Closing Date, each in customary form and substance satisfactory to the Underwriters and Underwriters\' Counsel. Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company will serve as transfer agent and registrar for the Common Stock.

**[5. Expenses]{.underline}**

**Company Expenses.** The Company shall bear all costs and expenses incurred in connection with the Offering, including but not limited to: SEC registration fees; printing and distribution of the Registration Statement, Preliminary Prospectus, and Final Prospectus; Financial Industry Regulatory Authority, Inc. (\"FINRA\") filing fees; Nasdaq Global Select Market listing fees; transfer agent fees; blue sky qualification and filing fees; road show expenses (including travel, venue rental, and presentation materials); and the fees and disbursements of Company Counsel (Stonebridge & Calloway LLP). The estimated total Company expenses are approximately \$3,200,000. The FINRA filing fee is estimated at \$21,780, based on the maximum aggregate offering amount.

**Underwriter Expenses.** The Underwriters shall bear their own counsel fees and out-of-pocket expenses incurred in connection with the Offering, including fees and disbursements of Underwriters\' Counsel (Ashford & Pine LLP). The estimated Underwriter expenses are approximately \$850,000.

**Selling Stockholder Expenses.** Each Selling Stockholder shall bear its own legal counsel fees incurred in connection with its participation in the Offering. The Company shall reimburse any transfer taxes attributable to the sale of shares by the Selling Stockholders in the Offering, if applicable.

**Expense Reimbursement upon Non-Consummation.** In the event the Offering is not consummated for any reason other than a material breach by Hargrove of its obligations under this Letter or the Underwriting Agreement, the Company shall reimburse Hargrove for its reasonable, documented out-of-pocket expenses incurred in connection with the Offering, including fees and disbursements of Underwriters\' Counsel, up to a maximum aggregate amount of \$500,000. Such reimbursement shall be payable within 30 days of Hargrove\'s written demand therefor, accompanied by reasonable supporting documentation.

**[6. Conditions to Underwriters\' Obligations]{.underline}**

The obligation of the Underwriters to purchase the Firm Shares (and any Over-Allotment Option shares) shall be subject to the satisfaction (or waiver by the Lead Book-Runner) of the following conditions, which will be set forth in greater detail in the definitive Underwriting Agreement:

> \(a\) The Registration Statement on Form S-1 shall have been declared effective by the SEC under the Securities Act, and no stop order suspending the effectiveness thereof shall have been issued and no proceedings for that purpose shall have been initiated or threatened by the SEC.
>
> \(b\) No stop order shall have been issued, and no proceedings for the issuance of a stop order shall have been initiated or, to the knowledge of the Company, threatened by the SEC.
>
> \(c\) The shares of Common Stock to be delivered at the Closing Date shall have been approved for listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market, subject only to official notice of issuance.
>
> \(d\) The Underwriting Agreement shall have been duly executed and delivered by all parties thereto.
>
> \(e\) The Underwriters shall have received a comfort letter from Whitman Reese & Co., dated as of the date of the Final Prospectus, and a bring-down comfort letter dated as of the Closing Date, in each case in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Lead Book-Runner and Underwriters\' Counsel.
>
> \(f\) The Underwriters shall have received (i) a legal opinion and negative assurance letter from Company Counsel, Stonebridge & Calloway LLP, and (ii) a legal opinion from Underwriters\' Counsel, Ashford & Pine LLP, each dated as of the Closing Date and in form and substance satisfactory to the Lead Book-Runner.
>
> \(g\) The Underwriters shall have received a certificate signed by the Chief Executive Officer and the Chief Financial Officer of the Company, dated as of the Closing Date, in customary form.
>
> \(h\) Lock-up agreements shall have been executed and delivered by all directors, officers, and holders of 1% or more of the pre-IPO outstanding shares of Common Stock, in form and substance satisfactory to Hargrove.
>
> \(i\) There shall not have occurred any material adverse change in the business, properties, financial condition, or results of operations of the Company since the date of the latest financial statements included in the Registration Statement.
>
> \(j\) The Offering shall comply with all applicable FINRA rules, including Rule 5110 (underwriting compensation shall not exceed 8% of gross offering proceeds) and Rule 5121 (no FINRA-disqualifying conflict of interest). Hargrove Securities LLC, CRD No. 87542, and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc., CRD No. 63291, shall each have confirmed compliance therewith.
>
> \(k\) The Underwriters shall have received a \"cold comfort\" letter regarding FINRA compliance from Company Counsel or such other counsel as the Lead Book-Runner may reasonably request.

**[7. Lock-Up Provisions]{.underline}**

All directors, executive officers, and holders of 1% or more of the outstanding shares of Common Stock (on a pre-IPO basis) of the Company shall execute lock-up agreements (each, a \"Lock-Up Agreement\") in form and substance satisfactory to Hargrove.

**Lock-Up Period.** The standard lock-up period shall be 180 days from the date of the Final Prospectus (the \"Lock-Up Period\").

**Restricted Activities.** During the Lock-Up Period, each locked-up party shall not, directly or indirectly, (i) offer, sell, contract to sell, or otherwise dispose of any shares of Common Stock or securities convertible into or exercisable or exchangeable for Common Stock, (ii) grant any option, right, or warrant to purchase Common Stock or such convertible securities, (iii) enter into any swap, hedge, or other arrangement that transfers the economic consequences of ownership of Common Stock, or (iv) pledge or otherwise encumber any shares of Common Stock, whether any such transaction is to be settled by delivery of Common Stock, in cash, or otherwise.

**Customary Exceptions.** The lock-up restrictions shall be subject to customary exceptions, including: (a) bona fide gifts to family members or trusts established for estate planning purposes, provided that the donee or transferee agrees in writing to be bound by the Lock-Up Agreement; (b) transfers pursuant to a pre-existing Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established prior to the date of the Final Prospectus; and (c) transfers by operation of law (including pursuant to a qualified domestic relations order).

**Early Release.** Hargrove may, in its sole discretion, release any party from the restrictions of its Lock-Up Agreement at any time prior to the expiration of the Lock-Up Period; provided, however, that if such release involves shares representing more than 1% of the outstanding shares of Common Stock immediately following the Offering, Hargrove shall provide the Company with at least three (3) business days\' prior written notice of such release.

The terms of the Lock-Up Agreements, including any additional or supplemental restrictions applicable to specific stockholders, will be set forth in the definitive Underwriting Agreement and the form of Lock-Up Agreement attached thereto.

**[8. Representations and Warranties (Overview)]{.underline}**

The definitive Underwriting Agreement will contain customary representations and warranties from the Company and the Selling Stockholders, including but not limited to the following:

**Company Representations and Warranties:**

> \(a\) *Organization and Good Standing.* The Company is a corporation duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of the State of Delaware and is qualified to do business in each jurisdiction where the nature of its business or the ownership of its properties so requires.
>
> \(b\) *Authorization.* The Company has full corporate power and authority to execute and deliver the Underwriting Agreement and to consummate the transactions contemplated thereby.
>
> \(c\) *Capitalization.* The Company\'s authorized capital stock consists of 200,000,000 shares of Common Stock and 10,000,000 shares of preferred stock, par value \$0.001 per share. As of the date hereof, approximately 42,000,000 shares of Common Stock are issued and outstanding on a pre-IPO basis.
>
> \(d\) *Validity of Shares.* The Primary Shares and Over-Allotment Option shares, when issued and delivered, will be duly authorized, validly issued, fully paid, and nonassessable.
>
> \(e\) *Registration Statement.* The Registration Statement and the Prospectus do not and will not contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state any material fact required to be stated therein or necessary in order to make the statements therein, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading.
>
> \(f\) *Financial Statements.* The financial statements included in the Registration Statement present fairly the financial condition and results of operations of the Company and have been prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles (\"GAAP\") consistently applied, as audited by Whitman Reese & Co.
>
> \(g\) *No Material Adverse Change.* Since the date of the latest audited financial statements included in the Registration Statement, there has been no material adverse change in the business, properties, financial condition, or results of operations of the Company.
>
> \(h\) *Compliance with Laws.* The Company is in compliance with all applicable laws, rules, and regulations, including applicable regulations of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (\"FDA\").
>
> \(i\) *Intellectual Property.* The Company owns or has the right to use all intellectual property material to the conduct of its business, including 14 issued U.S. utility patents, 7 pending patent applications, and FDA 510(k) clearance K230847 for the PulseGuard Pro device.
>
> \(j\) *Material Contracts.* All material contracts of the Company, including the supply agreement with Tanaka Precision Components Ltd. and the license agreement with the University of Texas at Austin, are in full force and effect and the Company is not in material breach thereof.
>
> \(k\) *Litigation.* Except as disclosed in the Registration Statement, including the patent infringement action filed by VitaBand Corp. (Case No. 6:24-cv-00891, U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Texas) and the pending EEOC charge, there is no material litigation pending or, to the knowledge of the Company, threatened against the Company.
>
> \(l\) *Tax Matters.* The Company has filed all material tax returns required to be filed and has paid all material taxes due.
>
> \(m\) *Nasdaq Listing.* The Company satisfies the listing requirements of the Nasdaq Global Select Market.

**Selling Stockholder Representations and Warranties:**

> \(a\) Each Selling Stockholder has valid title to the shares to be sold by it and the power and authority to sell, transfer, and deliver such shares.
>
> \(b\) The sale of shares by each Selling Stockholder has been duly authorized and does not conflict with any agreement or instrument to which such Selling Stockholder is a party or by which it is bound.
>
> \(c\) All information furnished by each Selling Stockholder for inclusion in the Registration Statement is accurate and complete in all material respects.

**[9. Indemnification]{.underline}**

The indemnification provisions set forth below will be reflected in the definitive Underwriting Agreement.

**Company Indemnification of Underwriters.** The Company shall indemnify and hold harmless each Underwriter, its respective directors, officers, employees, and agents, and each person who controls such Underwriter within the meaning of Section 15 of the Securities Act or Section 20 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the \"Exchange Act\"), against any and all losses, claims, damages, or liabilities (or actions in respect thereof), joint or several, to which such indemnified party may become subject, insofar as such losses, claims, damages, or liabilities arise out of or are based upon (i) any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in the Registration Statement, any Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, or (ii) any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading; except insofar as such losses, claims, damages, or liabilities arise out of or are based upon any untrue statement or omission made in reliance upon and in conformity with information furnished in writing by the Underwriters specifically for use in the Registration Statement, any Preliminary Prospectus, or the Final Prospectus.

**Selling Stockholder Indemnification of Underwriters.** Each Selling Stockholder shall, severally and not jointly, indemnify and hold harmless each Underwriter, its respective directors, officers, employees, and agents, and each person who controls such Underwriter within the meaning of Section 15 of the Securities Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act, against any and all losses, claims, damages, or liabilities (or actions in respect thereof) to which such indemnified party may become subject, insofar as such losses, claims, damages, or liabilities arise out of or are based upon any material misstatement or omission in the Registration Statement, any Preliminary Prospectus, or the Final Prospectus relating to such Selling Stockholder. The Company and the Underwriters shall be entitled to rely upon each Selling Stockholder\'s indemnification obligations set forth herein without limitation as to the source of the information giving rise to such misstatement or omission, provided that it relates to such Selling Stockholder.

**Underwriter Indemnification of Company and Selling Stockholders.** Each Underwriter shall, severally and not jointly, indemnify and hold harmless the Company, its directors, its officers who sign the Registration Statement, and each Selling Stockholder, against any and all losses, claims, damages, or liabilities arising out of any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in, or any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated in, the Registration Statement, any Preliminary Prospectus, or the Final Prospectus, but only to the extent that such untrue statement or omission was made in reliance upon and in conformity with information furnished in writing by such Underwriter specifically for use therein.

**Contribution.** If the indemnification provided for above is unavailable or insufficient to hold harmless an indemnified party, the indemnifying party shall contribute to the amount paid or payable as a result of such losses, claims, damages, or liabilities in such proportion as is appropriate to reflect the relative benefits received by the Company (measured by the net proceeds from the Primary Shares), the Selling Stockholders (measured by the net proceeds from their respective Secondary Shares), and the Underwriters (measured by the Underwriting Discount and commissions received) from the Offering. If allocation based on relative benefits is not permitted by applicable law, contribution shall be based on relative fault.

**Cap on Selling Stockholder Liability.** Each Selling Stockholder\'s aggregate indemnification and contribution obligations under this Section 9 shall not exceed the net proceeds actually received by such Selling Stockholder from the sale of its shares in the Offering.

**[10. Exclusivity]{.underline}**

During the term of this engagement, the Company agrees not to engage or authorize any other investment bank, broker-dealer, or financial intermediary to act as lead book-runner or lead manager for the Offering without the prior written consent of Hargrove. This exclusivity provision does not restrict the Company from engaging financial advisors for purposes unrelated to the Offering, including mergers and acquisitions advisory, debt financing, or other capital markets transactions not involving the Offering.

The exclusivity period shall commence on the date hereof and shall continue through the earlier of (i) the Closing Date and (ii) the termination of this Letter in accordance with Section 12.

**[11. Confidentiality]{.underline}**

Each party hereto agrees to keep confidential the terms and existence of this Letter and all non-public information received from the other party in connection with the Offering (collectively, \"Confidential Information\"), and shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party, subject to the following exceptions:

> \(a\) disclosures required by applicable law, regulation, subpoena, or other legal process (provided that the disclosing party shall, to the extent legally permitted, provide the other party with prompt written notice of such requirement);
>
> \(b\) disclosures to directors, officers, employees, counsel, accountants, and other professional advisors of the disclosing party on a need-to-know basis, provided that such persons are bound by confidentiality obligations no less restrictive than those set forth herein; and
>
> \(c\) information that becomes publicly available other than as a result of a breach of this Letter.

The confidentiality obligations set forth in this Section 11 shall survive any termination of this Letter.

**[12. Termination]{.underline}**

This engagement may be terminated by either party upon 30 days\' prior written notice to the other party, delivered in accordance with Section 14. Hargrove may terminate this engagement immediately upon written notice to the Company upon the occurrence of (i) a material adverse change in the business, properties, financial condition, or results of operations of the Company, or (ii) a material breach by the Company of any term, covenant, or condition of this Letter that remains uncured for ten (10) business days following written notice from Hargrove specifying such breach.

Notwithstanding any termination of this Letter, the provisions of Section 5 (Expenses), Section 9 (Indemnification), Section 11 (Confidentiality), and Section 14 (Governing Law and Miscellaneous) shall survive such termination and remain in full force and effect.

In the event of termination prior to the completion of the Offering, the expense reimbursement provisions of Section 5, including the \$500,000 cap on reimbursable expenses, shall apply in accordance with their terms.

**[13. Regulatory Matters]{.underline}**

Hargrove Securities LLC is a registered broker-dealer with the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority, Inc. (FINRA), CRD No. 87542. Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc. is a registered broker-dealer with FINRA, CRD No. 63291. Each of the Underwriters is a member in good standing of FINRA and the Securities Investor Protection Corporation (SIPC).

The Offering will be conducted in compliance with all applicable rules and regulations, including the Securities Act of 1933, the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, and all applicable FINRA rules, including FINRA Rule 5110 (Corporate Financing Rule --- Underwriting Terms and Arrangements) and FINRA Rule 5121 (Public Offerings of Securities with Conflicts of Interest).

Total underwriting compensation in connection with the Offering shall not exceed 8.0% of the gross offering proceeds, in compliance with FINRA Rule 5110. No FINRA-disqualifying conflict of interest exists under FINRA Rule 5121 with respect to either Hargrove or Bellweather.

The Underwriters will file the required documents and information with FINRA in connection with the Offering. The estimated FINRA filing fee of \$21,780, calculated based on the maximum aggregate offering amount, will be borne by the Company as set forth in Section 5 of this Letter.

**[14. Governing Law and Miscellaneous]{.underline}**

**Governing Law.** This Letter shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to the conflict of laws principles thereof.

**Jurisdiction.** Any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Letter shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in the Borough of Manhattan, City of New York, State of New York, and each party hereby irrevocably submits to the personal jurisdiction of such courts and waives any objection to venue therein.

**Entire Agreement.** This Letter, together with any exhibits or annexes attached hereto, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations, representations, warranties, commitments, offers, contracts, and undertakings of any nature with respect thereto.

**Amendments.** This Letter may not be amended, modified, or supplemented except by a written instrument duly executed by both parties hereto.

**Counterparts.** This Letter may be executed in one or more counterparts (including by means of facsimile or portable document format (.pdf) signature pages), each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument.

**Notices.** All notices, requests, demands, and other communications under this Letter shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been duly given when (i) delivered by hand, (ii) sent by registered or certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, (iii) sent by nationally recognized overnight courier service, or (iv) transmitted by electronic mail with confirmation of receipt, to the parties at the addresses set forth in the header of this Letter, or to such other address as a party may designate in writing from time to time.

**Assignment.** Neither party may assign its rights or obligations under this Letter without the prior written consent of the other party; provided, however, that Hargrove may assign its rights and obligations hereunder to an affiliate of Hargrove without the consent of the Company, provided that Hargrove shall remain liable for the performance of such obligations.

**No Third-Party Beneficiaries.** Except for the indemnified parties expressly described in Section 9, this Letter is not intended to, and shall not, create any rights or obligations in favor of any person or entity other than the parties hereto.

**Waiver of Jury Trial.** EACH PARTY HEREBY IRREVOCABLY WAIVES, TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, ANY AND ALL RIGHTS TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY ACTION, SUIT, OR PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS LETTER OR THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED HEREBY.

**[15. Acceptance]{.underline}**

We look forward to working with Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc. on this important transaction. If the foregoing correctly sets forth our agreement, please indicate your acceptance by signing and returning the enclosed copy of this letter.

Very truly yours,

HARGROVE SECURITIES LLC

(\"**Bellweather Capital**\" and, together with Hargrove Securities, the \"**Underwriters**\") propose to enter into an Underwriting Agreement (the \"**Underwriting Agreement**\") on or about March 19, 2025, with Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the \"**Company**\"), and certain selling stockholders named therein, providing for the initial public offering (the \"**Offering**\") of shares of common stock, par value \$0.001 per share, of the Company (the \"**Common Stock**\"), pursuant to a Registration Statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333-284517) filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the \"**SEC**\"). A final prospectus relating to the Offering (the \"**Final Prospectus**\") is to be filed by the Company with the SEC pursuant to Rule 424(b)(4) under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the \"**Securities Act**\"). The undersigned acknowledges that the Underwriters are entering into the Underwriting Agreement and proceeding with the Offering in reliance upon this Lock-Up Agreement.

In consideration of the foregoing, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the undersigned agrees as follows:

**[1. Lock-Up Restrictions]{.underline}**

During the period beginning on the date of the Final Prospectus and ending one hundred eighty (180) days after the date of the Final Prospectus (the \"**Lock-Up Period**\"), the undersigned will not, without the prior written consent of Hargrove Securities LLC (which consent may be withheld in its sole discretion):

> \(a\) offer, sell, contract to sell, pledge, grant any option to purchase, make any short sale, or otherwise transfer or dispose of, directly or indirectly, any shares of Common Stock or any securities convertible into, exercisable for, or exchangeable for shares of Common Stock (including, without limitation, shares of Common Stock that may be deemed to be beneficially owned by the undersigned in accordance with the rules and regulations of the SEC and securities that may be issued upon exercise of a stock option or warrant), whether now owned or hereafter acquired by the undersigned (collectively, the \"**Lock-Up Securities**\");
>
> \(b\) enter into any swap, hedge, or other arrangement that transfers to another, in whole or in part, any of the economic consequences of ownership of the Lock-Up Securities, whether any such transaction is to be settled by delivery of Common Stock or other securities, in cash, or otherwise;
>
> \(c\) make any demand for, or exercise any right with respect to, the registration under the Securities Act of any Lock-Up Securities; or
>
> \(d\) publicly disclose the intention to do any of the foregoing.

The foregoing restrictions shall apply whether any such transaction described above is to be settled by delivery of Common Stock or such other securities, in cash, or otherwise.

**[2. Permitted Exceptions]{.underline}**

The restrictions set forth in Section 1 shall not apply to:

> \(a\) **Transfers as bona fide gifts or charitable contributions.** Transfers of Lock-Up Securities made as bona fide gifts or charitable contributions, provided that the donee or recipient agrees in writing to be bound by the terms of this Lock-Up Agreement and no filing under Section 16(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the \"**Exchange Act**\"), reporting a reduction in beneficial ownership shall be required or voluntarily made during the Lock-Up Period (other than a filing on Form 5 made after the expiration of the Lock-Up Period);
>
> \(b\) **Transfers to family members or trusts for estate planning.** Transfers of Lock-Up Securities to any member of the undersigned\'s immediate family or to a trust for the direct or indirect benefit of the undersigned or the undersigned\'s immediate family, provided that such transferee agrees in writing to be bound by the terms of this Lock-Up Agreement and no filing under Section 16(a) of the Exchange Act reporting a reduction in beneficial ownership shall be required or voluntarily made during the Lock-Up Period (other than a filing on Form 5). For purposes of this Lock-Up Agreement, \"**immediate family**\" means any relationship by blood, marriage, or adoption, not more remote than first cousin;
>
> \(c\) **Transfers by operation of law.** Transfers of Lock-Up Securities by operation of law, such as pursuant to a qualified domestic relations order, divorce decree, or intestacy, provided that the transferee agrees in writing to be bound by this Lock-Up Agreement;
>
> \(d\) **Transfers to affiliates or members (for entities).** If the undersigned is a corporation, partnership, limited liability company, or other entity, distributions or transfers of Lock-Up Securities to members, partners, stockholders, or affiliates of the undersigned, provided that each distributee or transferee agrees in writing to be bound by this Lock-Up Agreement and no filing under Section 16(a) of the Exchange Act reporting a reduction in beneficial ownership shall be required or voluntarily made during the Lock-Up Period;
>
> \(e\) **Transactions pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 plan established before the IPO.** Transactions effected pursuant to a trading plan established prior to the date of the Underwriting Agreement and meeting the requirements of Rule 10b5-1(c) under the Exchange Act, provided that (i) such plan was established prior to the date hereof, (ii) such plan does not provide for the transfer of Lock-Up Securities during the Lock-Up Period, and (iii) no public announcement or filing under the Exchange Act regarding such transactions shall be required or voluntarily made during the Lock-Up Period. For the avoidance of doubt, the undersigned shall not enter into any new Rule 10b5-1 plan or modify any existing plan during the Lock-Up Period without the prior written consent of Hargrove Securities LLC;
>
> \(f\) **Exercise of options / settlement of RSUs.** The exercise of stock options or the settlement of restricted stock units granted under the Company\'s equity incentive plans, provided that the underlying shares of Common Stock received upon such exercise or settlement shall remain subject to the restrictions of this Lock-Up Agreement. The undersigned may also engage in \"sell-to-cover\" transactions or withholding of shares by the Company solely to satisfy tax withholding obligations arising from such exercise or settlement, provided that any shares sold are sold only to satisfy such tax obligations and that any required filings under Section 16(a) of the Exchange Act clearly indicate in the footnotes thereto that the sale was solely to cover tax withholding obligations;
>
> \(g\) **Shares sold in the Offering.** The sale of shares of Common Stock by the undersigned pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement, if the undersigned is a Selling Stockholder thereunder; and
>
> \(h\) **Transfers to the Company.** Transfers of Lock-Up Securities to the Company in connection with the repurchase of shares upon termination of employment or service, or in connection with the exercise of options on a \"net exercise\" or \"cashless\" basis.

All permitted transfers described in this Section 2 are subject to the condition that no voluntary public filing or report under the Exchange Act shall be required or made in connection with such transfer during the Lock-Up Period, except as specifically noted above.

**[3. Early Release Mechanics]{.underline}**

Hargrove Securities LLC may, in its sole discretion, and at any time and from time to time, release any or all of the Lock-Up Securities from the restrictions set forth herein, in whole or in part. If Hargrove Securities LLC determines to release Lock-Up Securities representing, in the aggregate, more than one percent (1%) of the Company\'s then-outstanding shares of Common Stock from the restrictions of this Lock-Up Agreement (other than releases in connection with transfers described in Section 2 above), Hargrove Securities LLC shall provide the Company with at least three (3) business days\' prior written notice of such release. The Company shall, promptly upon receipt of such notice (and in any event within one (1) business day), announce the impending release by press release through a major news service or through a filing with the SEC on a current report on Form 8-K. Any release granted to one or more signatories to a lock-up agreement shall not, by itself, entitle any other signatory to a release.

**[4. Stop Transfer Instructions]{.underline}**

The undersigned agrees that, prior to the expiration of the Lock-Up Period, the Company\'s transfer agent, Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company, or any successor transfer agent, is hereby authorized to decline to transfer, and the undersigned hereby consents to the entry of stop transfer instructions with respect to, any Lock-Up Securities subject to this Lock-Up Agreement. The undersigned further agrees that the Company may cause each certificate (if any) representing Lock-Up Securities to bear a legend referencing this Lock-Up Agreement. If the Lock-Up Securities are held in book-entry form through The Depository Trust Company, the undersigned agrees that appropriate restrictions may be placed on such book-entry securities.

**[5. Representations and Warranties of the Undersigned]{.underline}**

The undersigned represents and warrants that:

> \(a\) The undersigned has full power and authority to enter into this Lock-Up Agreement.
>
> \(b\) This Lock-Up Agreement has been duly authorized, executed, and delivered by the undersigned and constitutes the valid and binding obligation of the undersigned, enforceable against the undersigned in accordance with its terms, except as enforcement may be limited by applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, moratorium, or similar laws affecting creditors\' rights generally and by general equitable principles.
>
> \(c\) The execution, delivery, and performance of this Lock-Up Agreement by the undersigned does not and will not conflict with, result in a breach or violation of, or constitute a default under any agreement, contract, or instrument to which the undersigned is a party or by which the undersigned or any of the undersigned\'s property is bound.
>
> \(d\) All Lock-Up Securities beneficially owned by the undersigned as of the date hereof are listed on the signature page hereto.

**[6. Termination]{.underline}**

This Lock-Up Agreement shall automatically terminate and the undersigned shall be released from all obligations hereunder upon the earliest of:

> \(a\) the expiration of the Lock-Up Period (i.e., one hundred eighty (180) days after the date of the Final Prospectus);
>
> \(b\) the date on which the Underwriting Agreement is terminated prior to the sale of any shares of Common Stock thereunder (other than a termination following the closing of the Offering);
>
> \(c\) the date on which the Company notifies the Underwriters, or the Underwriters notify the Company, in writing, prior to the execution of the Underwriting Agreement, that the Offering has been abandoned; or
>
> \(d\) March 19, 2025, if the Underwriting Agreement has not been executed on or before such date.

**[7. Miscellaneous Provisions]{.underline}**

**(a) Governing Law.** This Lock-Up Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to conflicts of law principles thereof that would result in the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction.

**(b) Entire Agreement.** This Lock-Up Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings, whether written or oral, relating to such subject matter.

**(d) Binding Effect; Successors and Assigns.** This Lock-Up Agreement shall be binding upon the undersigned and the heirs, personal representatives, successors, and assigns of the undersigned. This Lock-Up Agreement shall inure to the benefit of Hargrove Securities LLC, Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc., and the Company and their respective successors and assigns.

**(e) Counterparts; Electronic Signatures.** This Lock-Up Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original. Signatures delivered by facsimile or electronic transmission (including .pdf) shall be deemed original signatures for all purposes.

**(f) Headings.** Section headings herein are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the interpretation of this Lock-Up Agreement.

**(g) Severability.** If any provision of this Lock-Up Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the validity, legality, and enforceability of the remaining provisions shall not be affected.

**(h) Notices.** All notices hereunder shall be in writing and delivered by hand, by nationally recognized overnight courier service, or by email (with confirmation of receipt) to: (i) if to Hargrove Securities LLC, at 405 Park Avenue South, 22nd Floor, New York, NY 10016, Attention: Cameron Whitfield, Managing Director, ECM; (ii) if to the Company, at 4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800, Austin, TX 78759, Attention: Samantha Reeves, General Counsel & Secretary; or (iii) if to the undersigned, at the address set forth on the signature page hereto.

CONFIDENTIAL

**WHITMAN REESE & CO.** **Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm** 200 S. Wacker Drive, Suite 1500 Chicago, IL 60606

March 18, 2025

Hargrove Securities LLC 405 Park Avenue South, 22nd Floor New York, NY 10016

Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc. 150 Federal Street, Suite 3100 Boston, MA 02110

> as Representatives of the several Underwriters named in the Underwriting Agreement

**Re: Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc. --- Registration Statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333-284517)**

Ladies and Gentlemen:

**[I. Purpose and Scope]{.underline}**

This letter is furnished pursuant to Section 7(d) of the Underwriting Agreement to be dated March 19, 2025 (the \"Agreement\"), among Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the \"Company\"), the Selling Stockholders named therein, and the several Underwriters named therein, for whom Hargrove Securities LLC and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc. are acting as Representatives. This letter is being provided in accordance with Statement on Auditing Standards No. 72, Letters for Underwriters and Certain Other Requesting Parties (AU-C Section 920), as issued by the Auditing Standards Board of the National Institute of Certified Public Accountants.

This letter is solely for the use of the addressees in connection with the proposed initial public offering (the \"Offering\") of shares of common stock, par value \$0.001 per share (the \"Common Stock\"), of the Company, and is not to be used, circulated, quoted, or otherwise referred to for any other purpose, whether within or outside the underwriting group.

**This letter is furnished in draft form.** The final comfort letter will be delivered at or prior to the time of pricing of the Offering, and a bring-down comfort letter will be delivered at or prior to the closing of the Offering, in each case in accordance with the terms of the Agreement.

The Registration Statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333-284517), initially filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the \"Commission\") on January 15, 2025, as amended by Amendment No. 1 filed February 10, 2025 and Amendment No. 2 filed March 3, 2025, and declared effective by the Commission on March 12, 2025 (together with all amendments thereto, the \"Registration Statement\"), and the related preliminary prospectus dated March 12, 2025 (the \"Preliminary Prospectus\"), have been reviewed by us for purposes of this letter. As the final prospectus supplement has not yet been filed with the Commission, all references herein to the \"Prospectus\" shall mean the Preliminary Prospectus as included in the Registration Statement as of the date the Registration Statement was declared effective.

**[II. Independence]{.underline}**

We are an independent registered public accounting firm with respect to the Company within the meaning of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the \"Act\"), and the applicable rules and regulations thereunder adopted by the Commission and the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (the \"PCAOB\"). Our independence determination has been made in accordance with Rule 2-01 of Regulation S-X promulgated by the Commission and PCAOB Rule 3520, *Auditor Independence*. We confirm that we are independent with respect to Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc. under Rule 2-01 of Regulation S-X promulgated by the Commission and under the rules of the PCAOB.

**[III. Audit Report and Financial Statements]{.underline}**

We have audited the consolidated balance sheets of Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc. and its subsidiaries (collectively, the \"Company\") as of December 31, 2024 and December 31, 2023, and the related consolidated statements of operations, comprehensive income, stockholders\' equity, and cash flows for each of the three years in the period ended December 31, 2024 (the \"Audited Financial Statements\"). Our audit report, dated February 28, 2025, with respect to the Audited Financial Statements is included in the Registration Statement. Our audit was conducted in accordance with the standards of the PCAOB. Our audit report expresses an unqualified opinion on the Audited Financial Statements.

The Audited Financial Statements and the related notes thereto, and the financial statement schedules, included in the Registration Statement are covered by our audit report. Among the amounts set forth in the Audited Financial Statements that we confirmed in connection with our audit procedures are the following:

> • Total revenue for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024: \$287,400,000
>
> • Net income for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024: \$18,600,000

No audit procedures were performed subsequent to February 28, 2025, the date of our audit report on the Audited Financial Statements, except as described in this letter with respect to the Change Period (as defined in Section VI below).

**[IV. Unaudited Interim Financial Information]{.underline}**

The Registration Statement includes unaudited condensed consolidated financial statements of the Company as of September 30, 2024 and for the nine-month periods ended September 30, 2024 and September 30, 2023 (the \"Interim Financial Statements\"). We performed a review of the Interim Financial Statements in accordance with PCAOB Auditing Standard 4105, *Reviews of Interim Financial Information*.

A review of interim financial information is substantially less in scope than an audit conducted in accordance with the standards of the PCAOB and does not provide a basis for the expression of an opinion on the financial statements taken as a whole. Our review report on the Interim Financial Statements, dated November 15, 2024, is included in the Registration Statement and contains no modifications.

Certain unaudited interim financial data derived from the Interim Financial Statements is included in the Prospectus Summary and Selected Financial Data sections of the Prospectus, as further described in Section V below and in Appendix A hereto.

**[V. Agreed-Upon Financial Information Procedures]{.underline}**

At your request, we have performed the following procedures, which were agreed to by the addressees of this letter, with respect to certain financial information included in the Registration Statement. These procedures, which were applied as indicated below, do not constitute an audit or review made in accordance with PCAOB standards.

**A. Prospectus Summary Financial Data**

We have confirmed that the following amounts set forth in the Prospectus Summary under \"Summary Consolidated Financial Data\" agree with or were derived from the Audited Financial Statements or the Interim Financial Statements included in the Registration Statement:

> \(i\) Total revenue for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024: \$287,400,000 --- agrees with the audited consolidated statement of operations.
>
> \(ii\) Net income for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024: \$18,600,000 --- agrees with the audited consolidated statement of operations.
>
> \(iii\) Total assets as of December 31, 2024: \$312,700,000 --- agrees with the audited consolidated balance sheet.
>
> \(iv\) Total stockholders\' equity as of December 31, 2024: \$198,400,000 --- agrees with the audited consolidated balance sheet.
>
> \(v\) Total long-term debt (including current portion) as of December 31, 2024: \$22,500,000 --- agrees with the audited consolidated balance sheet (representing the outstanding balance under the revolving credit facility with Oakvale National Bank).
>
> \(vi\) Cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2024: \$47,300,000 --- agrees with the audited consolidated balance sheet.

**B. Selected Consolidated Financial Data**

We have confirmed that the selected three-year consolidated financial data for the fiscal years ended December 31, 2022, December 31, 2023, and December 31, 2024, presented in the Registration Statement under \"Selected Consolidated Financial Data,\" agree with or were derived from the audited consolidated financial statements for the respective years, which financial statements were audited by us.

**C. Capitalization Table**

We have confirmed that the amounts in the Capitalization table under the \"Actual\" column agree with or were derived from the audited consolidated balance sheet as of December 31, 2024:

> \(i\) Common stock, \$0.001 par value, 200,000,000 shares authorized; 42,000,000 shares issued and outstanding --- agrees.
>
> \(ii\) Preferred stock, \$0.001 par value, 10,000,000 shares authorized; no shares issued and outstanding --- agrees.
>
> \(iii\) Revolving credit facility: \$22,500,000 outstanding --- agrees.
>
> \(iv\) Total stockholders\' equity: \$198,400,000 --- agrees.

With respect to the \"As Adjusted\" column in the Capitalization table, which reflects pro forma adjustments to give effect to the Offering, we have confirmed the arithmetic accuracy of such adjustments:

> \(i\) Post-offering shares outstanding (prior to exercise of the overallotment option): 42,000,000 + 8,000,000 = 50,000,000.
>
> \(ii\) Post-offering shares outstanding (assuming full exercise of the overallotment option): 50,000,000 + 1,800,000 = 51,800,000.
>
> \(iii\) Net proceeds to the Company from the sale of primary shares (at assumed initial public offering price of \$24.00 per share, after underwriting discount of \$1.44 per share): 8,000,000 × \$22.56 = \$180,480,000.

**D. Dilution.** We have confirmed that the data set forth in the Dilution section of the Prospectus was derived from or agrees with the Audited Financial Statements and the offering assumptions as described therein. With respect to stock option data included in the Dilution section, we confirmed:

> \(i\) Employee stock options outstanding: 4,200,000 options with a weighted-average exercise price of \$6.50 per share --- agrees with Note 12 to the Audited Financial Statements.
>
> \(ii\) Shares reserved for future grants under the 2024 Equity Incentive Plan: 1,800,000 shares --- agrees with Note 12 to the Audited Financial Statements.

**E. Statistical and Market Data**

We have compared certain statistical data (including percentages, ratios, and per share data) set forth in the Registration Statement to the underlying audited or reviewed financial statements and have confirmed the arithmetic accuracy of the computations of such data. We note that we have made no independent verification of any non-financial statistical or market data from third-party sources cited in the Registration Statement.

**[VI. Subsequent Changes --- Negative Assurance (Change Period)]{.underline}**

At your request, we have read the minutes of meetings of the Board of Directors and committees thereof of the Company through March 10, 2025, and have made inquiries of certain officials of the Company who have responsibility for financial and accounting matters as to whether, during the period from January 1, 2025 through March 10, 2025 (the \"Change Period\"), there were any changes in the Company\'s consolidated financial position or results of operations.

Based on our reading of the minutes and our inquiries as described above (which do not constitute an audit or review in accordance with PCAOB standards), nothing came to our attention that caused us to believe that, during the Change Period:

> \(i\) there was any change in the capital stock (other than issuances of shares of common stock upon the exercise of stock options in the ordinary course) or long-term debt of the Company and its consolidated subsidiaries, or any decrease in consolidated net current assets or consolidated stockholders\' equity, as compared to the respective amounts shown in the December 31, 2024 audited consolidated balance sheet included in the Registration Statement; or
>
> \(ii\) there was any decrease in consolidated revenues, consolidated net income, or consolidated net income per share (basic or diluted), for the period from January 1, 2025 through March 10, 2025, as compared to the corresponding period of the preceding fiscal year;

except in each case for changes, increases, or decreases that the Registration Statement discloses have occurred or may occur or that are described in this letter.

We note that our procedures with respect to the Change Period extended only through March 10, 2025, and we have not performed any procedures relating to the period after March 10, 2025. Accordingly, this letter does not address any changes, events, or developments that may have occurred after March 10, 2025.

The date of this letter is March 18, 2025. The procedures described in this section were completed on or about March 10, 2025, which is the latest date to which the Change Period extends.

We understand that, pursuant to the Agreement, a bring-down comfort letter will be required to be delivered at the time of closing. We expect that the bring-down comfort letter will update the negative assurance set forth above through a date no more than three business days prior to the date of such bring-down comfort letter. We will, as part of the bring-down comfort letter procedures, extend our review of the Change Period through such later date.

**[VII. Specific Agreed-Upon Financial Items --- Additional Procedures]{.underline}**

At the request of the addressees, we have performed the following additional specific procedures:

**A. Revolving Credit Facility**

We have confirmed that the outstanding balance of \$22,500,000 under the revolving credit facility with Oakvale National Bank as of December 31, 2024 agrees with the Audited Financial Statements. We have further confirmed that the \$40,000,000 commitment amount referenced in the Registration Statement agrees with the summary of the credit agreement reviewed in connection with our audit. We did not audit or review the terms of the credit agreement itself, including any financial covenants, mandatory prepayment provisions, or events of default, and we express no opinion thereon.

**B. Stock Option and Equity Incentive Plan Data**

We have confirmed that the following data set forth in the Registration Statement agree with the Company\'s stock option records as of December 31, 2024 and with Note 12 to the Audited Financial Statements:

> \(i\) Options outstanding: 4,200,000.
>
> \(ii\) Weighted-average exercise price: \$6.50 per share.
>
> \(iii\) Shares reserved for future grants under the 2024 Equity Incentive Plan: 1,800,000.
>
> \(iv\) The 2024 Equity Incentive Plan was adopted by the Board of Directors on November 15, 2024 and approved by stockholders on December 20, 2024, as disclosed in Note 12 to the Audited Financial Statements.

**C. Federal Net Operating Loss Carryforwards**

We have confirmed that the amount of \$14,200,000 in federal net operating loss carryforwards as of December 31, 2024 disclosed in the Registration Statement agrees with Note 15 to the Audited Financial Statements and is consistent with information provided to us by the Company from its federal income tax returns for the applicable periods.

**D. Pro Forma Financial Data (Arithmetic Accuracy)**

We have confirmed the arithmetic accuracy of the pro forma calculations of net proceeds and per-share data in the Registration Statement, including the following:

> \(i\) Gross proceeds at assumed initial public offering price of \$24.00 per share for 8,000,000 primary shares offered by the Company: \$192,000,000.
>
> \(ii\) Underwriting discount: \$1.44 per share × 8,000,000 shares = \$11,520,000.
>
> \(iii\) Estimated offering expenses payable by the Company: \$3,200,000.
>
> \(iv\) Net proceeds to the Company (primary shares, prior to exercise of overallotment option): \$192,000,000 less \$11,520,000 underwriting discount less \$3,200,000 estimated offering expenses = \$177,280,000.

We verified the arithmetic accuracy only of the foregoing computations. We express no opinion on the assumptions or estimates underlying the pro forma calculations or on the reasonableness thereof.

**[VIII. Procedures Regarding Non-Financial Statement Information]{.underline}**

Certain information in the Registration Statement of a non-financial statement nature, including but not limited to the description of the Company\'s business, industry and market data, risk factors, and the narrative portions of \"Management\'s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations,\" has not been audited, reviewed, or subjected to agreed-upon procedures by Whitman Reese & Co.

We have not audited, reviewed, compiled, or applied agreed-upon procedures to any financial statements of the Company as of any date or for any period subsequent to December 31, 2024, except for the limited procedures described in Sections IV and VI of this letter. We make no representations regarding the adequacy of disclosures or the fairness of any information in the Registration Statement not covered by our audit report or review report.

This letter should not be taken to supplant procedures that the addressees should perform in making their own assessment of, and investment decision regarding, the Offering.

**[IX. Limitations and Restrictions on Use]{.underline}**

This letter is furnished solely for the benefit of the addressees named herein and solely in connection with the initial public offering of Common Stock of the Company as described in the Registration Statement. This letter is not to be used, circulated, quoted, or otherwise referred to within or without the underwriting group for any other purpose, including but not limited to any filing with any governmental or regulatory agency or any securities exchange.

This letter is not intended to be, and should not be, relied upon by any other party, including the Company, the Selling Stockholders, or any investor or prospective investor in the Common Stock. Our responsibility in connection with this letter is solely to the addressees and is limited to the procedures specified herein.

The procedures described herein would not necessarily reveal all matters of significance to the addressees, and the procedures should not be taken to supplant any additional inquiries or procedures that the addressees would undertake in connection with the Offering.

This letter is furnished in **draft** form. The form and substance of the final comfort letter, to be dated as of the pricing date of the Offering, and the bring-down comfort letter, to be dated as of the closing date, may differ from this draft to reflect changes in circumstances, the passage of time, and the extension of the Change Period through a more current date. Whitman Reese & Co. has no obligation to update this letter for events or circumstances occurring after the date hereof.

**[X. Conclusion]{.underline}**

This letter is furnished to you solely for use in connection with the proposed public offering described above and should not be used, quoted, or referred to in whole or in part for any other purpose.

Very truly yours,

WHITMAN REESE & CO. Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Gregory Whitman, CPA Engagement Partner

200 S. Wacker Drive, Suite 1500 Chicago, Illinois 60606

March 18, 2025
