**[POWER OF ATTORNEY AND CUSTODY AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**Relating to the Initial Public Offering of Common Stock of Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc.**

**Dated as of March \_\_, 2025**

This Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement (this \"**Agreement**\") is a form document to be executed individually by each Selling Stockholder identified herein in connection with the initial public offering of common stock of Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc. This Agreement is expected to be executed on or prior to the Pricing Date of March 19, 2025.

**[RECITALS]{.underline}**

**WHEREAS**, Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the \"**Company**\"), with principal offices at 4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800, Austin, Texas 78759, proposes to file a Registration Statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333-284517) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the \"**Commission**\") under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the \"**Securities Act**\"), relating to the initial public offering (the \"**Offering**\") of shares of the Company\'s common stock, par value \$0.001 per share (the \"**Common Stock**\");

**WHEREAS**, the Offering will include the sale of shares of Common Stock by certain stockholders of the Company (each, a \"**Selling Stockholder**\"), as described in the Registration Statement and the related Prospectus;

**WHEREAS**, the undersigned Selling Stockholder (the \"**Stockholder**\") desires to sell shares of Common Stock in the Offering and wishes to appoint attorneys-in-fact and a custodian to act on the Stockholder\'s behalf in connection therewith;

**WHEREAS**, the underwriters of the Offering are expected to be led by Hargrove Securities LLC, as lead book-running manager, and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc., as co-manager (collectively, the \"**Underwriters**\");

**WHEREAS**, the Stockholder desires to irrevocably appoint David Nishimura, Chief Financial Officer of the Company, and Samantha Reeves, General Counsel and Secretary of the Company, as the Stockholder\'s attorneys-in-fact and custodians for the purposes set forth herein; and

**WHEREAS, the Selling Stockholders consist of Cascade Kestridge Ventures, LP, a Delaware limited partnership, Northlight Growth Partners Fund II, LP, a Delaware limited partnership, and Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy, an individual, each of whom executes a separate copy of this Agreement.**

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the premises and the mutual covenants and agreements contained herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Stockholder hereby agrees as follows:

**[1. Appointment of Attorneys-in-Fact]{.underline}**

The Stockholder hereby irrevocably constitutes and appoints David Nishimura and Samantha Reeves, and each of them individually (the \"**Attorneys-in-Fact**\"), as the Stockholder\'s true and lawful attorneys-in-fact, with full power of substitution and resubstitution, for the Stockholder and in the Stockholder\'s name, place, and stead, to act on behalf of the Stockholder in connection with the Offering, with full power and authority to do and perform each and every act and thing requisite and necessary to be done in connection therewith, as fully and to all intents and purposes as the Stockholder might or could do in person.

This appointment constitutes a durable power of attorney coupled with an interest and shall not be revoked by the death, disability, or incapacity of the Stockholder, or, in the case of an entity, the dissolution or termination of the Stockholder. This appointment is irrevocable and shall survive and remain in full force and effect until the earlier of: (a) the completion of the Closing (and any Additional Closing relating to the exercise of the over-allotment option) under the Underwriting Agreement; or (b) the termination of the Underwriting Agreement without any sale of shares having occurred.

**[2. Scope of Authority]{.underline}**

The Stockholder hereby grants to the Attorneys-in-Fact full power and authority to take any and all of the following actions on behalf of and in the name of the Stockholder:

> \(a\) Execute and deliver, on behalf of and in the name of the Stockholder, the underwriting agreement (the \"**Underwriting Agreement**\") among the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Underwriters, in such form as the Attorneys-in-Fact shall approve;
>
> \(b\) Execute and deliver any amendments, supplements, or modifications to the Underwriting Agreement;
>
> \(c\) Execute and deliver any lock-up agreement or market standoff agreement required by the Underwriters in connection with the Offering, including a lock-up agreement providing for a 180-day restricted period from the date of the Final Prospectus (and, in the case of Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy, inclusive of the springing extension provision described in the applicable term sheet);
>
> \(d\) Deliver or cause to be delivered the shares of Common Stock to be sold by the Stockholder (the \"**Stockholder Shares**\") pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement, including by directing the Transfer Agent, Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company, to transfer such shares in accordance with the terms of the Underwriting Agreement;
>
> \(e\) Receive and receipt for the net proceeds from the sale of the Stockholder Shares, after deduction of the underwriting discount and any applicable transfer taxes, and deposit such proceeds in accordance with the Stockholder\'s written instructions;
>
> \(f\) Execute and deliver selling stockholder questionnaires, certificates, and other documents required in connection with the Registration Statement, the Underwriting Agreement, or the Offering;
>
> \(g\) Furnish information in writing on behalf of the Stockholder for inclusion in the Registration Statement and Prospectus, subject to the Stockholder\'s prior written approval of the specific information to be furnished;
>
> \(h\) Determine the number of Stockholder Shares to be sold in the Offering, consistent with the maximum number specified in Schedule A hereto;
>
> \(i\) Execute, deliver, and file any documents required in connection with applicable federal, state, or foreign securities laws; and
>
> \(j\) Take all other actions and execute all other documents as may be necessary or desirable to consummate the sale of the Stockholder Shares in the Offering.

**[3. Custody of Shares]{.underline}**

The Stockholder hereby deposits with the Attorneys-in-Fact, acting as custodians (in such capacity, the \"**Custodians**\"), certificates or book-entry positions representing the Stockholder Shares, or agrees to cause such shares to be delivered to or held by the Custodians or the Transfer Agent (Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company) for the benefit of the Custodians, promptly following execution of this Agreement.

The Custodians shall hold the Stockholder Shares in custody for the purposes of delivery to the Underwriters at the Closing (expected to be March 24, 2025, or such other date as determined pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement) and any Additional Closing related to the exercise of the over-allotment option.

If the Stockholder Shares are held in certificated form, the Stockholder shall deliver stock certificates endorsed in blank or accompanied by stock powers duly executed in blank, with signatures guaranteed, to the Custodians. If the Stockholder Shares are held in book-entry form, the Stockholder shall cause Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company to hold such shares subject to the instructions of the Custodians.

The Custodians shall not release or deliver the Stockholder Shares except: (i) to the Underwriters at the Closing or any Additional Closing pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement; (ii) back to the Stockholder if the Underwriting Agreement is terminated prior to any Closing without any sale of shares having occurred; or (iii) as otherwise directed by the Stockholder and the Attorneys-in-Fact jointly in writing.

**[4. Number of Shares; Schedule A]{.underline}**

The maximum number of Stockholder Shares to be sold by the Stockholder in the Offering is as set forth on **Schedule A** attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference. The specific number of shares to be sold, the public offering price per share, the underwriting discount per share, and the net proceeds to the Stockholder per share shall be as set forth in the Underwriting Agreement as executed by the Attorneys-in-Fact on behalf of the Stockholder.

The Stockholder hereby authorizes the Attorneys-in-Fact to agree to a reduction, but not an increase, of the number of Stockholder Shares from the maximum number set forth on Schedule A, if necessary or appropriate in connection with the Offering. Schedule A sets forth the share information for each Selling Stockholder: Cascade Kestridge Ventures, LP --- up to 2,000,000 shares; Northlight Growth Partners Fund II, LP --- up to 1,500,000 shares; and Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy --- up to 500,000 shares.

**[5. Distribution of Proceeds]{.underline}**

Upon receipt of the net proceeds from the sale of the Stockholder Shares (calculated as the public offering price per share less the underwriting discount per share of \$1.44, less any applicable transfer taxes), the Custodians shall distribute such proceeds to the Stockholder in accordance with the Stockholder\'s written wire transfer instructions provided to the Custodians prior to the Closing Date.

The Custodians shall deduct from the Stockholder\'s net proceeds any transfer taxes, if any, payable by the Stockholder in connection with the sale of the Stockholder Shares; provided, however, that the Company has agreed, pursuant to the terms of the Offering, to reimburse transfer taxes attributable to Selling Stockholder shares, if any.

The Custodians shall have no obligation to invest or reinvest any proceeds held temporarily pending distribution to the Stockholder and shall not be liable for any interest or earnings on proceeds so held.

**[6. Representations and Warranties of the Stockholder]{.underline}**

The Stockholder hereby represents and warrants to the Attorneys-in-Fact and the Company as follows:

> \(a\) **Authority.** The Stockholder has full right, power, and authority to enter into this Agreement and to sell, assign, transfer, and deliver the Stockholder Shares. If the Stockholder is an entity, it is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of its jurisdiction of organization, and the execution and delivery of this Agreement and the consummation of the transactions contemplated hereby have been duly authorized by all necessary action on the part of the Stockholder.
>
> \(b\) **Title to Shares.** The Stockholder is the record and beneficial owner of the shares of Common Stock set forth on Schedule A, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, equities, and claims, and has full right and authority to sell and deliver such shares. Upon delivery of and payment for the Stockholder Shares pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement, the Underwriters will receive valid title to such shares, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, equities, and claims.
>
> \(c\) **No Conflicts.** The execution and delivery of this Agreement and the sale of the Stockholder Shares will not conflict with, result in a breach of, or constitute a default under: (i) any agreement, indenture, or instrument to which the Stockholder is a party or by which the Stockholder is bound; (ii) the organizational documents of the Stockholder (if an entity); or (iii) any order, judgment, or decree of any court or governmental authority having jurisdiction over the Stockholder.
>
> \(d\) **No Registration Rights Violation.** The sale of the Stockholder Shares does not and will not violate any registration rights agreement or similar agreement to which the Stockholder is a party.
>
> \(e\) **Selling Stockholder Information.** All information furnished or to be furnished by the Stockholder in writing expressly for use in the Registration Statement (File No. 333-284517), the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto is and will be true, correct, and complete in all material respects and does not and will not contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading.
>
> \(f\) **No Manipulation.** The Stockholder has not taken and will not take, directly or indirectly, any action designed to, or that might reasonably be expected to, cause or result in stabilization or manipulation of the price of the Common Stock in violation of applicable law.
>
> \(g\) **Compliance with Lock-Up.** The Stockholder will execute and deliver a lock-up agreement in the form required by the lead Underwriter, Hargrove Securities LLC, and will comply with all restrictions on transfer set forth therein for the duration of the applicable restricted period.
>
> \(h\) **No Brokers.** No broker, finder, or similar agent has been employed by or on behalf of the Stockholder in connection with the Offering, and no person is entitled to any commission, fee, or other compensation from the Stockholder in connection with the sale of the Stockholder Shares, other than the underwriting discount payable to the Underwriters.

**[7. Duties and Liabilities of Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians]{.underline}**

The Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians shall not be liable for any action taken or omitted to be taken in good faith in connection with this Agreement, except for their own willful misconduct or gross negligence. The Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians are acting solely as agents of the Stockholder and shall have no fiduciary or other duty to the Stockholder beyond the specific duties expressly set forth in this Agreement. The Attorneys-in-Fact shall act in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the Underwriting Agreement.

The Stockholder acknowledges that David Nishimura serves as Chief Financial Officer and Samantha Reeves serves as General Counsel and Secretary of the Company, and that each is also acting on behalf of the Company and other Selling Stockholders in connection with the Offering. The Stockholder hereby waives any conflict of interest arising therefrom. The Attorneys-in-Fact shall have no obligation to take any action that they reasonably believe to be contrary to applicable law.

**[8. Indemnification of Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians]{.underline}**

The Stockholder agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians from and against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees and expenses) arising out of or relating to the performance of their duties under this Agreement, except to the extent that such losses, claims, damages, liabilities, or expenses result from the willful misconduct or gross negligence of the Attorneys-in-Fact or Custodians. This indemnification obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement.

**[9. Irrevocability]{.underline}**

This Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement is coupled with an interest and is irrevocable. It shall not be revoked by the death, disability, incapacity, dissolution, liquidation, or termination of the Stockholder, or by any other event. This Agreement may be terminated only upon mutual written agreement of the Stockholder and the Attorneys-in-Fact, or automatically upon the earlier of: (a) the completion of all closings under the Underwriting Agreement, including any closing related to the exercise of the over-allotment option (the deadline for exercise of which is April 23, 2025); or (b) the termination of the Underwriting Agreement without any closing having occurred.

**[10. Successors and Assigns]{.underline}**

This Agreement shall be binding upon the Stockholder and the Stockholder\'s heirs, executors, administrators, legal representatives, successors, and assigns. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the Attorneys-in-Fact and their successors. The Attorneys-in-Fact may not assign their obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the Stockholder; provided, however, that the Company may designate a successor to serve in the capacity of Attorney-in-Fact if either David Nishimura or Samantha Reeves ceases to be an officer of the Company, upon written notice to the Stockholder.

**[11. Governing Law]{.underline}**

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to conflict-of-laws principles thereof. Any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the federal and state courts located in the Borough of Manhattan, City of New York, and each party hereby irrevocably consents to the personal jurisdiction of such courts.

**[12. Notices]{.underline}**

All notices, requests, demands, and other communications under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered to the parties at the following addresses:

> If to the Attorneys-in-Fact / Custodians:
>
> c/o Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc. 4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800 Austin, Texas 78759
>
> Attention: David Nishimura, Chief Financial Officer
>
> and Samantha Reeves, General Counsel
>
> If to the Stockholder:
>
> At the address set forth on the signature page hereto.

Notices shall be deemed given upon personal delivery, one (1) business day after deposit with a nationally recognized overnight courier service, or three (3) business days after mailing by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid.

**[13. Miscellaneous]{.underline}**

**Entire Agreement.** This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, relating to such subject matter.

**Amendments.** This Agreement may not be amended, modified, or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by the Stockholder and the Attorneys-in-Fact.

**Severability.** If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision hereof, and the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

**Counterparts.** This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Electronic signatures and PDF copies of executed counterparts shall be treated as originals for all purposes.

**Headings.** Section headings contained in this Agreement are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the meaning or interpretation of this Agreement.

**Third-Party Beneficiaries.** The Underwriters, Hargrove Securities LLC and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc., are intended third-party beneficiaries of this Agreement with respect to the obligation of the Stockholder to deliver the Stockholder Shares and the authority of the Attorneys-in-Fact to execute the Underwriting Agreement on behalf of the Stockholder.

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

presence, and relationship consistency (no dangling rIds). Does NOT do XSD
schema validation in v1 — defer until ECMA-376 XSDs are vendored fresh from
ECMA-International.

Usage: python validate.py file.docx
Exit 0 = valid; non-zero = errors printed to stderr.
"""
import sys
import zipfile
from pathlib import Path
from lxml import etree


def validate(path: Path) -> list[str]:
    errors = []

    if not path.exists():
        return [f"File not found: {path}"]
    if not zipfile.is_zipfile(path):
        return [f"Not a valid ZIP: {path}"]

    with zipfile.ZipFile(path) as z:
        names = set(z.namelist())

        if "[Content_Types].xml" not in names:
            errors.append("Missing [Content_Types].xml")
            return errors

        # XML well-formedness for every .xml and .rels part
        for name in names:
            if name.endswith(".xml") or name.endswith(".rels"):
                try:
                    etree.fromstring(z.read(name))
                except etree.XMLSyntaxError as e:
                    errors.append(f"Malformed XML in {name}: {e}")

        # Relationship target consistency

**Firm-Commitment Underwriting Agreement**

**Initial Public Offering of Common Stock of Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc.**

---

Dated as of **March 19, 2025**

**MERIDIAN PULSE TECHNOLOGIES, INC.**, a corporation duly organized and existing under the laws of the State of Delaware (the "Company"),

**CASCADE KESTRIDGE VENTURES, LP**, a Delaware limited partnership,

**NORTHLIGHT GROWTH PARTNERS FUND II, LP**, a Delaware limited partnership, and

**DR. ANAND KRISHNAMURTHY**, an individual,

(each of the foregoing selling stockholders, individually, a "Selling Stockholder" and, collectively, the "Selling Stockholders"), and

**HARGROVE SECURITIES LLC** and **BELLWEATHER CAPITAL MARKETS, INC.**

(each an "Underwriter" and, collectively, the "Underwriters"),

each of the several Underwriters named in **Schedule B** hereto, for whom Hargrove Securities LLC is acting as representative (in such capacity, the "Representative" or the "Lead Book-Runner"),

confirm their agreement with each other as follows:

## 1. Definitions

As used in this Agreement, the following terms have the meanings set forth below:

"**Act**" means the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and the rules and regulations of the Commission thereunder.

"**Additional Closing**" means a closing of the sale of Option Securities pursuant to Section 3 hereof.

"**Bring-Down Comfort Letter**" means the bring-down comfort letter described in Section 7(b)(ii) hereof.

"**Closing**" means the initial closing of the sale of the Firm Securities pursuant to Section 2.4 hereof.

"**Closing Date**" means March 24, 2025, or such other date as the parties may agree, on which the Firm Securities are to be delivered against payment therefor.

"**Commission**" means the Securities and Exchange Commission.

"**Common Stock**" means the common stock, par value $0.001 per share, of the Company.

"**Company Counsel**" means Stonebridge & Calloway LLP.

"**Company Shares**" means the 8,000,000 shares of Common Stock to be sold by the Company pursuant to this Agreement, as set forth in Schedule B.

"**Comfort Letter**" means the comfort letter described in Section 7(b)(i) hereof.

"**Exchange Act**" means the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations of the Commission thereunder.

"**FINRA**" means the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority, Inc.

"**Final Prospectus**" means the final prospectus relating to the Securities, as filed with the Commission pursuant to Rule 424(b)(4) under the Act.

"**Firm Securities**" means the Company Shares and the Selling Stockholder Shares, collectively.

"**Free Writing Prospectus**" has the meaning set forth in Rule 405 under the Act.

"**Issuer Free Writing Prospectus**" means any Free Writing Prospectus that is an issuer free writing prospectus, as defined in Rule 433 under the Act.

"**Lock-Up Agreement**" means a lock-up agreement substantially in the form of **Exhibit A** hereto.

"**Material Adverse Effect**" means a material adverse change in the business, properties, management, financial condition, or results of operations of the Company and its subsidiaries, taken as a whole, or a material impairment of the ability of the Company to perform its obligations under this Agreement.

"**Nasdaq**" means the Nasdaq Global Select Market.

"**Option Securities**" means the 1,800,000 shares of Common Stock that the Underwriters have the option to purchase pursuant to Section 3 hereof.

"**Preliminary Prospectus**" means the preliminary prospectus included in the Registration Statement at the time it became effective, including all documents incorporated by reference therein.

"**Public Offering Price**" means $24.00 per share, being the initial public offering price per share at which the Firm Securities are sold to the public.

"**Registration Statement**" means the registration statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333-284517) filed by the Company with the Commission under the Act, as amended, including all documents incorporated by reference therein and all exhibits thereto, as declared effective by the Commission on March 12, 2025.

"**Representative**" means Hargrove Securities LLC, acting as representative of the several Underwriters.

"**Selling Stockholder Shares**" means the 4,000,000 shares of Common Stock to be sold by the Selling Stockholders pursuant to this Agreement, as set forth in Schedule A and Schedule B.

"**Securities**" means the Firm Securities and the Option Securities, collectively.

"**Time of Sale**" means the time at which the Public Offering Price is determined and this Agreement is executed.

"**Transfer Agent**" means Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company.

"**Underwriting Discount**" means 6.0% of the Public Offering Price per share, or $1.44 per share.

"**Underwriters' Counsel**" means Ashford & Pine LLP.

## 2. Sale and Purchase of Firm Securities

### 2.1 Sale and Purchase of Firm Securities

Upon the terms and subject to the conditions set forth herein, the Company agrees to sell to the several Underwriters, and each of the Underwriters, upon the basis of the several representations, warranties, and agreements of the Company and the Selling Stockholders contained herein and subject to the conditions set forth herein, agrees, severally and not jointly, to purchase from the Company the number of Company Shares set forth opposite such Underwriter's name in Schedule B, and each of the Selling Stockholders agrees to sell to the several Underwriters, and each of the Underwriters agrees, severally and not jointly, to purchase from such Selling Stockholder the number of Selling Stockholder Shares set forth opposite such Underwriter's name in Schedule B, in each case at the purchase price per share equal to the Public Offering Price less the Underwriting Discount (i.e., $22.56 per share).

### 2.2 Firm Commitment

The Underwriters are committed to purchase all of the Firm Securities if they purchase any of the Firm Securities. The obligations of the Underwriters to purchase the Firm Securities are several and not joint. The Company and the Selling Stockholders acknowledge and agree that the Underwriters are purchasing the Firm Securities on a firm-commitment basis.

### 2.3 Public Offering Price; Underwriting Discount; Net Proceeds

(a) The Public Offering Price is $24.00 per share. The Underwriting Discount is $1.44 per share (6.0% of the Public Offering Price). The purchase price per share payable by the Underwriters to the Company and the Selling Stockholders is $22.56 per share (the Public Offering Price less the Underwriting Discount).

(b) The aggregate gross proceeds to the Company and the Selling Stockholders from the sale of the Firm Securities at the Public Offering Price are $288,000,000. The aggregate Underwriting Discount on the Firm Securities is $17,280,000. The aggregate net proceeds to the Company and the Selling Stockholders from the sale of the Firm Securities (before offering expenses) are $270,720,000, of which $180,480,000 is attributable to the Company Shares and $90,240,000 is attributable to the Selling Stockholder Shares.

(c) The net proceeds to the Company from the sale of the Company Shares, after deduction of the Underwriting Discount and the estimated offering expenses payable by the Company (approximately $3,200,000), are approximately $177,280,000. The net proceeds to each Selling Stockholder from the sale of its Selling Stockholder Shares, after deduction of the Underwriting Discount, are as set forth in Schedule A.

### 2.4 Delivery and Payment

Delivery of, and payment for, the Firm Securities shall be made at the offices of Underwriters' Counsel, Ashford & Pine LLP, 1231 Avenue of the Americas, 34th Floor, New York, New York 10020 (or remotely via the facilities of The Depository Trust Company) at 10:00 a.m., New York time, on the Closing Date, March 24, 2025, or at such other time and place as the Representative and the Company may agree. Payment for the Firm Securities shall be made to the Company and the Selling Stockholders by wire transfer in immediately available funds to accounts designated by the Company and the Selling Stockholders, respectively, against delivery of the Firm Securities to the Representative for the account of the several Underwriters. The Firm Securities shall be delivered through the facilities of The Depository Trust Company. Certificates (if any) for the Firm Securities shall be in such denominations and registered in such names as the Representative may request, and shall be made available for examination and packaging by the Representative not later than two business days prior to the Closing Date.

## 3. Over-Allotment Option

### 3.1 Grant of Option

In order to facilitate the distribution of the Firm Securities, the Company hereby grants to the Underwriters an option (the "Over-Allotment Option"), exercisable at any time and from time to time, in whole or in part, within thirty (30) calendar days after the Closing Date (i.e., through April 23, 2025), to purchase from the Company up to 1,800,000 additional shares of Common Stock (the "Option Securities"), equal to 15% of the Firm Securities, solely to cover over-allotments, if any. The Option Securities shall be newly issued shares of Common Stock issued by the Company. The Over-Allotment Option is sourced exclusively from the Company; no Selling Stockholder Shares are subject to the Over-Allotment Option.

### 3.2 Exercise of Option

The Over-Allotment Option may be exercised by written notice (which may be by email) from the Representative to the Company, which notice shall set forth the number of Option Securities to be purchased and the date and time of delivery and payment therefor (the "Additional Closing Date"), which shall be not later than five business days after the date of such notice. Upon exercise of the Over-Allotment Option, the Company shall be obligated to sell, and each Underwriter shall be obligated, severally and not jointly, to purchase, the number of Option Securities set forth opposite such Underwriter's name in Schedule B (or such other number as the Representative may determine to reflect any reduction in the number of Option Securities being purchased), at the purchase price per share equal to the Public Offering Price less the Underwriting Discount (i.e., $22.56 per share).

### 3.3 Delivery and Payment for Option Securities

Delivery of, and payment for, the Option Securities shall be made at the offices of Underwriters' Counsel (or remotely via the facilities of The Depository Trust Company) on the Additional Closing Date, in the same manner as provided in Section 2.4 with respect to the Firm Securities.

## 4. Representations and Warranties of the Company

The Company represents and warrants to each Underwriter, as of the date hereof and as of the Closing Date, as follows:

### 4.1 Organization and Good Standing

The Company is a corporation duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of the State of Delaware, and has full corporate power and authority to own its properties and conduct its business as described in the Registration Statement and to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement. The Company is duly qualified to do business as a foreign corporation in each jurisdiction in which the nature of its business or the ownership or leasing of its properties makes such qualification necessary, except where the failure to be so qualified would not, individually or in the aggregate, have a Material Adverse Effect.

### 4.2 Authorization; Enforceability

The Company has full corporate power and authority to execute and deliver this Agreement and to carry out its obligations hereunder. This Agreement has been duly authorized, executed, and delivered by the Company and constitutes the legal, valid, and binding obligation of the Company, enforceable against the Company in accordance with its terms, except as enforceability may be limited by applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, moratorium, or similar laws affecting creditors' rights generally and by general principles of equity.

### 4.3 Capitalization

The authorized capital stock of the Company consists of 200,000,000 shares of Common Stock, par value $0.001 per share, and 10,000,000 shares of preferred stock, par value $0.001 per share. As of the date hereof, 42,000,000 shares of Common Stock are issued and outstanding, and no shares of preferred stock are issued and outstanding. All of the outstanding shares of Common Stock have been duly authorized, validly issued, fully paid, and nonassessable, and were issued in compliance with applicable federal and state securities laws. Upon the issuance and delivery of the Company Shares and the Option Securities against payment therefor in accordance with this Agreement, such shares will be duly authorized, validly issued, fully paid, and nonassessable. The Company's amended and restated certificate of incorporation and amended and restated bylaws are in the form filed as exhibits to the Registration Statement.

### 4.4 No Conflict

### 4.5 Registration Statement and Prospectus

The Registration Statement is the most recent registration statement filed by the Company with the Commission for the registration of the Securities under the Act. The Registration Statement has been declared effective by the Commission under the Act. The Preliminary Prospectus, at the time it was filed, in the form included in the Registration Statement at the time it became effective, did not contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading. The Final Prospectus, at the time it is filed with the Commission pursuant to Rule 424(b)(4) under the Act, will not contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading. ### 4.6 Financial Statements

The Audited Financial Statements included in the Registration Statement present fairly, in all material respects, the consolidated financial position of the Company as of the dates and for the periods indicated, and have been prepared in accordance with United States generally accepted accounting principles applied on a consistent basis throughout the periods involved, and were audited by Whitman Reese & Co., an independent registered public accounting firm, in accordance with the standards of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board. The unaudited interim financial statements included in the Registration Statement present fairly, in all material respects, the information required to be set forth therein.

### 4.7 No Material Adverse Change

Since the date of the most recent audited financial statements included in the Registration Statement (December 31, 2024), (a) there has been no Material Adverse Effect, and (b) there has been no change in the capital stock, long-term indebtedness, or any decrease in net current assets or stockholders' equity of the Company, except as disclosed in the Registration Statement or in the Final Prospectus.

### 4.8 Intellectual Property

The Company owns or has the right to use all intellectual property material to the conduct of its business as described in the Registration Statement, including 14 issued U.S. utility patents and 7 pending patent applications, and the exclusive license agreement with the University of Texas at Austin for bioelectric signal processing technology. The Company is not in material breach of any such license agreement. The Company has obtained FDA 510(k) clearance (Clearance No. K230847) for its PulseGuard Pro cardiac rhythm monitoring device.

### 4.9 Material Contracts

Each material contract of the Company, including (a) the supply agreement with Tanaka Precision Components Ltd., (b) the exclusive license agreement with the University of Texas at Austin, and (c) the revolving credit facility with Oakvale National Bank, is in full force and effect, constitutes a legal, valid, and binding obligation of the Company enforceable in accordance with its terms, and the Company is not in material breach or default thereunder. The descriptions of such material contracts in the Registration Statement are accurate and complete in all material respects.

### 4.10 Litigation

Except as disclosed in the Registration Statement, including the patent infringement action brought by VitaBand Corp. (Case No. 6:24-cv-00891, U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Texas) and the pending EEOC charge of discrimination, there is no action, suit, proceeding, or investigation pending or, to the knowledge of the Company, threatened against the Company before any court or governmental authority that, if adversely determined, would have a Material Adverse Effect.

### 4.11 Compliance with Laws

The Company is in compliance in all material respects with all applicable laws, rules, and regulations, including applicable regulations of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and applicable federal and state securities laws.

### 4.12 Tax Matters

The Company has filed all material tax returns required to be filed and has paid all material taxes due, except for taxes being contested in good faith. The Company has disclosed in the Registration Statement approximately $14.2 million in federal net operating loss carryforwards as of December 31, 2024.

### 4.13 FINRA Affiliations

No officer, director, or holder of 5% or more of the Company's securities, and no Selling Stockholder, has any direct or indirect affiliation or association with any FINRA member, except as disclosed in the Registration Statement or to the Representative and Underwriters' Counsel.

### 4.14 Nasdaq Listing

The Common Stock has been approved for listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol "MPLS," subject to official notice of issuance, and the Company has complied with all applicable listing requirements.

### 4.15 Use of Proceeds

The intended use of the net proceeds from the sale of the Company Shares, as described in the "Use of Proceeds" section of the Final Prospectus, does not conflict with, and will be applied in a manner consistent with, the terms of the Company's material contracts, including the mandatory prepayment provisions of the revolving credit facility with Oakvale National Bank.

### 4.16 No Manipulation

## 5. Representations and Warranties of the Selling Stockholders

Each Selling Stockholder, severally and not jointly, represents and warrants to each Underwriter, as of the date hereof and as of the Closing Date, as follows:

The Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement executed by such Selling Stockholder has been duly authorized, executed, and delivered and constitutes the legal, valid, and binding obligation of such Selling Stockholder, and the attorneys-in-fact appointed thereunder have full power and authority to execute and deliver this Agreement on behalf of such Selling Stockholder.

### 5.3 No Conflict

### 5.4 Selling Stockholder Information

All information furnished in writing by such Selling Stockholder specifically for inclusion in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto is and will be true, correct, and complete in all material respects and does not and will not contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading.

### 5.5 Compliance with Securities Laws

Such Selling Stockholder is not, and at the Time of Sale will not be, an "underwriter" as defined in Section 2(a)(11) of the Act, other than as a selling stockholder. Such Selling Stockholder has not taken, and will not take, any action designed to, or that might reasonably be expected to, cause or result in the stabilization or manipulation of the price of the Common Stock in violation of applicable law.

### 5.6 No Registration Rights Violation

The sale of the Selling Stockholder Shares by such Selling Stockholder does not and will not violate any registration rights agreement or similar agreement to which such Selling Stockholder is a party.

## 6. Covenants and Agreements of the Company

The Company covenants and agrees with each Underwriter as follows:

### 6.1 Compliance with Securities Laws; Filing of Prospectus

The Company will comply with the Act and the Exchange Act and the rules and regulations thereunder, and will file the Final Prospectus with the Commission pursuant to Rule 424(b)(4) under the Act within the required timeframe following the Time of Sale. The Company will promptly advise the Representative, in writing, of (a) any amendment or supplement to the Registration Statement or the Final Prospectus that the Company proposes to file, (b) the issuance by the Commission of any stop order or other order suspending the effectiveness of the Registration Statement or any post-effective amendment thereto, and (c) the initiation or threat of any proceeding for any such purpose.

### 6.2 Free Writing Prospectuses

The Company has not taken and will not take any action that would result in the Underwriters being required to file any Free Writing Prospectus with the Commission that would not otherwise be required to be filed by the Underwriters.

### 6.3 Use of Proceeds

The Company will apply the net proceeds from the sale of the Company Shares substantially as described in the "Use of Proceeds" section of the Final Prospectus, including the repayment of indebtedness under the revolving credit facility with Oakvale National Bank in accordance with the mandatory prepayment provisions of the credit agreement governing such facility.

### 6.4 Listing

The Company will use its commercially reasonable efforts to cause the Common Stock to be listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market and to maintain such listing.

### 6.5 Transfer Agent

The Company will provide a transfer agent and registrar for the Common Stock, being Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company, satisfactory to the Representative, and will maintain a registrar and transfer agent for so long as required by the Act.

### 6.6 Reports to Underwriters

### 6.7 No Manipulation

The Company will not, directly or indirectly, take any action designed to, or that might reasonably be expected to, cause or result in the stabilization or manipulation of the price of the Common Stock to facilitate the sale or resale of the Securities in violation of applicable law.

## 7. Conditions to the Obligations of the Underwriters

The obligations of the several Underwriters to purchase and pay for the Firm Securities on the Closing Date, and the Option Securities on the Additional Closing Date, shall be subject to the accuracy of the representations and warranties of the Company and the Selling Stockholders as of the date hereof and as of the Closing Date (or Additional Closing Date), the performance by the Company and the Selling Stockholders of their obligations hereunder, and the satisfaction of the following additional conditions:

### 7.1 Registration Statement; No Stop Order

The Registration Statement shall have been declared effective by the Commission under the Act and shall remain effective, and no stop order suspending the effectiveness of the Registration Statement shall have been issued, and no proceedings for that purpose shall have been initiated or threatened by the Commission.

### 7.2 Filing of Final Prospectus

### 7.3 Comfort Letter

(a) The Underwriters shall have received from Whitman Reese & Co., the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, a comfort letter, dated as of the date hereof (the "Comfort Letter"), in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Representative and Underwriters' Counsel, containing statements and information of the type ordinarily included in accountants' "comfort letters" to underwriters with respect to the financial statements and certain financial information contained in the Registration Statement and the Final Prospectus.

(b) The Underwriters shall have received from Whitman Reese & Co. a bring-down comfort letter (the "Bring-Down Comfort Letter"), dated as of the Closing Date, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Representative and Underwriters' Counsel, stating that the conclusions set forth in the Comfort Letter remain true and correct as of the Closing Date, with such changes as may be necessary to reflect the passage of time and events occurring after the date of the Comfort Letter.

### 7.4 Opinions of Counsel

(a) The Underwriters shall have received the opinion, dated as of the Closing Date, of Company Counsel, Stonebridge & Calloway LLP, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Representative and Underwriters' Counsel, including a negative assurance letter with respect to the Registration Statement and the Final Prospectus, substantially in the form of **Exhibit D** hereto.

(b) The Underwriters shall have received the opinion, dated as of the Closing Date, of Underwriters' Counsel, Ashford & Pine LLP, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Representative, substantially in the form of **Exhibit E** hereto.

### 7.5 Officers' Certificate

The Underwriters shall have received a certificate, dated as of the Closing Date, signed by Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy, Chief Executive Officer of the Company, and David Nishimura, Chief Financial Officer of the Company, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Representative, substantially in the form of **Exhibit B** hereto, to the effect that the representations and warranties of the Company are true and correct as of the Closing Date, that the Company has performed all obligations required to be performed by it on or prior to the Closing Date, and that no Material Adverse Effect has occurred since December 31, 2024.

The Underwriters shall have received such additional certificates and documents as the Representative and Underwriters' Counsel may reasonably request to confirm the accuracy of the representations and warranties of the Company and the Selling Stockholders, the performance of their obligations hereunder, and the satisfaction of the conditions set forth herein.

## 8. Covenants and Agreements of the Selling Stockholders

Each Selling Stockholder covenants and agrees with the Underwriters as follows:

### 8.1 Delivery of Shares

Each Selling Stockholder shall cause its Selling Stockholder Shares to be delivered to the Underwriters at the Closing (and any Additional Closing, to the extent applicable) through the facilities of The Depository Trust Company, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, and restrictions on transfer (other than under the Securities Act), in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement executed by such Selling Stockholder.

Each Selling Stockholder shall execute and deliver a Lock-Up Agreement in the form of Exhibit A hereto and shall comply with all restrictions on transfer set forth therein for the duration of the applicable restricted period.

## 9. Indemnification and Contribution

### 9.1 Indemnification by the Company

The Company agrees to indemnify and hold harmless each Underwriter, its affiliates, directors, officers, employees, and agents, and each person, if any, who controls such Underwriter within the meaning of Section 15 of the Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act (each, an "Underwriter Indemnified Party"), against any and all losses, claims, damages, and liabilities (or actions in respect thereof), joint or several, to which such Underwriter Indemnified Party may become subject, under the Act, the Exchange Act, or otherwise, insofar as such losses, claims, damages, and liabilities (or actions in respect thereof) arise out of or are based upon (a) any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, or (b) any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading; provided, however, that the Company shall not be liable to the extent that any such loss, claim, damage, or liability arises out of or is based upon any untrue statement or omission made in reliance upon and in conformity with written information furnished to the Company by or on behalf of any Underwriter specifically for use in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto. The Company shall also reimburse each Underwriter Indemnified Party for any legal or other expenses reasonably incurred by it in connection with investigating, defending, or settling any such loss, claim, damage, liability, or action.

### 9.2 Indemnification by the Selling Stockholders

Each Selling Stockholder agrees, severally and not jointly, to indemnify and hold harmless each Underwriter Indemnified Party against any and all losses, claims, damages, and liabilities (or actions in respect thereof) to which such Underwriter Indemnified Party may become subject, under the Act, the Exchange Act, or otherwise, insofar as such losses, claims, damages, and liabilities (or actions in respect thereof) arise out of or are based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact, or any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated, in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, but only with respect to information furnished in writing by such Selling Stockholder specifically for inclusion in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, or the Final Prospectus. Each Selling Stockholder shall also reimburse each Underwriter Indemnified Party for any legal or other expenses reasonably incurred by it in connection with investigating, defending, or settling any such loss, claim, damage, liability, or action. The aggregate liability of each Selling Stockholder under this Section 9.2 shall not exceed the net proceeds actually received by such Selling Stockholder from the sale of its Selling Stockholder Shares in the Offering.

### 9.3 Indemnification by the Underwriters

Each Underwriter agrees, severally and not jointly, to indemnify and hold harmless the Company, its directors, each officer of the Company who signed the Registration Statement, each person, if any, who controls the Company within the meaning of Section 15 of the Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act, and each Selling Stockholder (each, a "Company Indemnified Party"), against any and all losses, claims, damages, and liabilities (or actions in respect thereof) to which such Company Indemnified Party may become subject, under the Act, the Exchange Act, or otherwise, insofar as such losses, claims, damages, and liabilities (or actions in respect thereof) arise out of or are based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact, or any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated, in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, but only to the extent that such untrue statement or omission was made in reliance upon and in conformity with written information furnished to the Company by or on behalf of such Underwriter specifically for use in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto. Each Underwriter shall also reimburse each Company Indemnified Party for any legal or other expenses reasonably incurred by it in connection with investigating, defending, or settling any such loss, claim, damage, liability, or action. The aggregate liability of the Underwriters under this Section 9.3 shall not exceed the total Underwriting Discount actually received by the Underwriters.

### 9.4 Contribution

If the indemnification provided for in Sections 9.1, 9.2, or 9.3 is unavailable or insufficient to hold harmless an indemnified party in respect of any losses, claims, damages, or liabilities (or actions in respect thereof) referred to therein, then each indemnifying party shall contribute to the aggregate amount of such losses, claims, damages, and liabilities (or actions in respect thereof) in such proportion as is appropriate to reflect the relative benefits received by the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Underwriters from the Offering. If allocation based on relative benefits is not permitted by applicable law, contribution shall be determined in such proportion as is appropriate to reflect not only the relative benefits but also the relative fault of the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Underwriters, on the one hand, and the indemnified party, on the other. The relative benefits received by the Company shall be deemed to be equal to the net proceeds from the Company Shares, the relative benefits received by the Selling Stockholders shall be deemed to be equal to the net proceeds from the Selling Stockholder Shares, and the relative benefits received by the Underwriters shall be deemed to be equal to the total Underwriting Discount. The aggregate contribution of the Underwriters shall not exceed the total Underwriting Discount actually received by the Underwriters. No Selling Stockholder shall be required to contribute an amount in excess of the net proceeds received by such Selling Stockholder from the sale of its Selling Stockholder Shares in the Offering. No person found guilty of fraudulent misrepresentation within the meaning of Section 11(f) of the Act shall be entitled to contribution from any person who was not guilty of such fraudulent misrepresentation.

### 9.5 Procedure

Promptly after receipt by an indemnified party of notice of the commencement of any action, such indemnified party shall, if a claim in respect thereof is to be made against an indemnifying party, notify the indemnifying party in writing of the commencement thereof; provided that the failure to so notify the indemnifying party shall not relieve it from any liability that it may have under this Section 9 except to the extent it is materially prejudiced thereby. The indemnifying party shall be entitled to participate in, and, to the extent it shall desire, to assume the defense of any such action with counsel reasonably satisfactory to the indemnified party. The indemnified party shall have the right to employ separate counsel in any such action, but the fees and expenses of such counsel shall be at the expense of the indemnified party unless (a) the indemnifying party has failed to assume the defense or employ counsel reasonably satisfactory to the indemnified party, or (b) the named parties to any such action include both the indemnified party and the indemnifying party and representation of both by the same counsel would be inappropriate due to actual or potential differing interests between them.

## 10. Termination

### 10.1 Termination by the Representative

The Representative, on behalf of the several Underwriters, may terminate this Agreement at any time prior to the Closing Date by notice to the Company and the Selling Stockholders if, in the reasonable judgment of the Representative, any of the following has occurred:

(a) any Material Adverse Effect that, in the reasonable judgment of the Representative, makes it impracticable or inadvisable to proceed with the Offering;

(b) any suspension or material limitation of trading in securities generally on the Nasdaq Stock Market, the New York Stock Exchange, or any other national securities exchange;

(c) any general moratorium on commercial banking activities in the United States or New York declared by federal or New York state authorities;

(d) any material disruption in securities settlement, payment, or clearance services in the United States;

(e) any outbreak or escalation of hostilities, declaration of war, national emergency, act of terrorism, or other calamity or crisis that, in the reasonable judgment of the Representative, materially and adversely affects United States financial markets; or

(f) any downgrade, or public notice of any intended or potential downgrade, of the rating of any securities of the Company or any of its subsidiaries by any nationally recognized statistical rating organization.

### 10.2 Effect of Termination

If this Agreement is terminated pursuant to Section 10.1, no party shall have any liability to any other party, except that (a) the Company shall remain liable for expenses as provided in Section 11, and (b) the indemnification and contribution provisions set forth in Section 9 shall survive any such termination. If this Agreement is terminated after the purchase of any Securities by the Underwriters, such Securities shall remain subject to this Agreement.

## 11. Expenses

The Company shall bear all expenses incurred in connection with the Offering, including SEC registration fees, FINRA filing fees, Nasdaq listing fees, printing and engraving expenses, transfer agent fees, blue sky qualification fees and expenses, road show expenses, the fees and expenses of Company Counsel (Stonebridge & Calloway LLP), and the accounting and auditing fees of Whitman Reese & Co. The estimated total Company offering expenses are approximately $3,200,000. The Underwriters shall bear their own counsel fees and out-of-pocket expenses, including the fees and expenses of Underwriters' Counsel (Ashford & Pine LLP), estimated at approximately $850,000. Each Selling Stockholder shall bear its own legal counsel fees. The Company shall reimburse any transfer taxes attributable to the Selling Stockholder Shares, if any.

## 12. Lock-Up Agreements

### 12.1 Standard Lock-Up

Each of the Company's directors, executive officers, and holders of 1% or more of the outstanding shares of Common Stock (on a pre-Offering basis) has executed or shall execute, prior to or at the Closing Date, a Lock-Up Agreement substantially in the form of Exhibit A hereto, pursuant to which such person agrees, for a period of 180 days from the date of the Final Prospectus (the "Lock-Up Period"), not to offer, sell, contract to sell, pledge, or otherwise dispose of or hedge any shares of Common Stock or securities convertible into or exchangeable for Common Stock, without the prior written consent of the Representative, subject to customary exceptions as set forth in the form of Lock-Up Agreement attached as Exhibit A.

### 12.2 Springing Extension for Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy

In addition to the standard 180-day Lock-Up Period, the Lock-Up Agreement executed by Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy shall include a springing market standoff extension provision, pursuant to which, if the closing price of the Common Stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market is below the Public Offering Price per share for five (5) consecutive trading days during the final seventeen (17) trading days of the standard 180-day Lock-Up Period, then Dr. Krishnamurthy's lock-up shall automatically extend by an additional eighteen (18) calendar days beyond the standard 180-day period, for a total lock-up of one hundred ninety-eight (198) days from the date of the Final Prospectus. This springing extension applies solely to Dr. Krishnamurthy and shall be subject to the same customary exceptions and early release mechanics set forth in the form of Lock-Up Agreement.

### 12.3 Early Release

The Representative may, in its sole discretion, release any locked-up party from the restrictions of its Lock-Up Agreement, in whole or in part, at any time. If the Representative determines to release Lock-Up Securities representing, in the aggregate, more than one percent (1%) of the Company's then-outstanding shares of Common Stock from the restrictions of a Lock-Up Agreement (other than releases in connection with permitted transfers described in the form of Lock-Up Agreement), the Representative shall provide the Company with at least three (3) business days' prior written notice of such release.

## 13. Miscellaneous

### 13.1 Governing Law

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to the conflict of laws principles thereof that would require the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction.

### 13.2 Jurisdiction; Waiver of Jury Trial

Each party irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in the Borough of Manhattan, City of New York, State of New York, in any action arising out of or relating to this Agreement, and waives any objection to venue therein. Each party irrevocably waives, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, any and all rights to trial by jury in any action arising out of or relating to this Agreement.

### 13.3 Notices

All notices, requests, demands, and other communications under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given when delivered by hand, sent by nationally recognized overnight courier, or sent by email with confirmation of receipt, to the parties at the following addresses (or such other address as a party may designate in writing):

**To the Company:**

Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc.
4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800
Austin, TX 78759
Attention: Samantha Reeves, General Counsel & Secretary

**With a copy to:**

Stonebridge & Calloway LLP
600 Congress Avenue, Suite 2400
Austin, TX 78701
Attention: Jonathan Calloway

**To the Representative:**

Hargrove Securities LLC
405 Park Avenue South, 22nd Floor
New York, NY 10016
Attention: Cameron Whitfield, Managing Director, Equity Capital Markets

**With a copy to:**

Ashford & Pine LLP
1231 Avenue of the Americas, 34th Floor

Attention: Victoria Ashford

**To the Co-Manager:**

Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc.

Attention: Patrick Donnelly, Managing Director

**To each Selling Stockholder:**

At the address set forth for such Selling Stockholder in Schedule A, or, with respect to the Selling Stockholder Shares, to the attorneys-in-fact appointed under the Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement, c/o Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc., 4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800, Austin, TX 78759, Attention: David Nishimura and Samantha Reeves.

### 13.4 Entire Agreement; Amendments

This Agreement, together with the Schedules and Exhibits hereto, constitutes the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, and negotiations, whether written or oral, with respect thereto. This Agreement may not be amended or modified except by a written instrument signed by the Company, the Selling Stockholders (or their attorneys-in-fact), and the Representative on behalf of the Underwriters.

### 13.5 Counterparts; Electronic Signatures

This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Signatures delivered by facsimile or electronic transmission (including .pdf) shall be deemed original signatures for all purposes.

### 13.6 Severability

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the validity, legality, and enforceability of the remaining provisions shall not be affected, and such provision shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable.

### 13.7 Headings

Section headings herein are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the interpretation of this Agreement.

### 13.8 Survival

The representations, warranties, covenants, and agreements contained herein shall survive the Closing and the delivery of, and payment for, the Securities. The obligations of the Company and the Selling Stockholders under Section 9 (Indemnification and Contribution) and Section 11 (Expenses) shall survive the termination of this Agreement.

### 13.9 Assignment

This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns. No party may assign its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other parties, except that the Representative may assign its rights and obligations hereunder to an affiliate of the Representative with the consent of the Company (not to be unreasonably withheld).

### 13.10 Representatives; Authority

The Representative is acting on behalf of the several Underwriters in connection with the Offering. Any action taken by the Representative in accordance with this Agreement shall be binding on all of the Underwriters. The Company and the Selling Stockholders acknowledge that the Underwriters are acting in their capacities as underwriters and not as financial advisors or fiduciaries to the Company or the Selling Stockholders.

### 13.11 Selling Stockholder Execution

Each Selling Stockholder has appointed David Nishimura and Samantha Reeves, and each of them, as its attorneys-in-fact pursuant to the Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement, to execute and deliver this Agreement on its behalf. The execution and delivery of this Agreement by such attorneys-in-fact on behalf of each Selling Stockholder shall be deemed the execution and delivery of this Agreement by such Selling Stockholder.

## 14. Closing Deliverables

At or prior to the Closing, the Company shall deliver, or cause to be delivered, to the Representative and Underwriters' Counsel the following:

(a) the Comfort Letter and the Bring-Down Comfort Letter;

(b) the opinion of Company Counsel and the negative assurance letter from Company Counsel;

(c) the opinion of Underwriters' Counsel;

(d) the Officers' Certificate;

(e) the Secretary's Certificate;

(f) executed Lock-Up Agreements from all required parties;

(g) the Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement from each Selling Stockholder;

(h) the completed selling stockholder questionnaire from each Selling Stockholder;

(i) evidence of Nasdaq listing approval;

(j) evidence of FINRA clearance; and

(k) such other documents as the Representative and Underwriters' Counsel may reasonably request.

## SCHEDULE B — UNDERWRITERS AND ALLOCATIONS

| Underwriter | Firm Securities Allocation | % of Firm | Option Securities Allocation | % of Option |
|---|---|---|---|---|

The Firm Securities allocation for each Underwriter consists of the number of shares set forth above, comprising both Company Shares and Selling Stockholder Shares, allocated among the Company and the Selling Stockholders in proportion to the 8,000,000 Company Shares and 4,000,000 Selling Stockholder Shares (i.e., two-thirds Company Shares and one-third Selling Stockholder Shares of each Underwriter's allocation).

## SCHEDULE C — LOCK-UP PARTIES

The following persons are required to execute Lock-Up Agreements pursuant to Section 12 hereof:

## EXHIBIT A — FORM OF LOCK-UP AGREEMENT
